summary: Iteration 5 — Frontend-only validation of the new Product JSON-LD AggregateRating + Review schema and the visible 'What customers are saying' section on product.php. Tested 3 product slugs (Bitdefender Mac 2 reviews 4.5, Office Home 2024 Mac 0 reviews, Office Home 2024 PC 2 reviews 4.5). All schema/UI assertions passed. Also re-ran the Buy Now → cart → checkout regression flow.

issues: Mixed-content warnings: order-success preview loads http://localhost/assets/images/brand/email-logo.gif and http://localhost/track-open.php?t=... over an HTTPS page. Browsers block these as insecure. Fix: build absolute URLs from SITE_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or use protocol-relative // prefix for the email-template assets.

action_items: (Minor) Fix mixed-content http://localhost/* asset references inside the order-success / email-template HTML so the test-mode preview iframe does not log Mixed Content warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: product.php lines 116-152 cleanly attach AggregateRating + Review only when stats.count > 0 — correct conditional; passes Google's Product rich-result spec (ratingValue, bestRating, worstRating, reviewCount all present; each Review has @type Person author, datePublished YYYY-MM-DD, reviewBody, reviewRating with ratingValue + bestRating + worstRating).
Single source of truth: $_reviewRows is built once from product_reviews() and reused for both JSON-LD serialization and visible section render — no duplicate DB hit.
Privacy trim is correctly applied (helper outputs 'First L.' patterns: Marcus L., Sarah J., Emily B., Dan W. — verified no full last names leak).
Schema-visibility parity: every reviewBody in JSON-LD is present (as a prefix of) the visible review-card comment text — satisfies Google's anti-review-stuffing requirement.
Zero-review path correctly omits both the schema keys and the visible section (microsoft-office-home-2024-mac confirmed clean).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on reviews-schema feature; checkout regression: navigation chain + form validation OK, test-mode order rendered successfully (POST /checkout.php → 302 not directly captured because the 'Preview test order' button opens an in-page demo iframe instead of a real POST; actual POST requires filling the Stripe iframe with a test card)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data; relied on the 11 published customer_reviews seeded in iteration_2.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reviews schema feature is fully validated for 3 product slugs (Bitdefender Mac, Office Home 2024 Mac, Office Home 2024 PC). Each test parsed the <script type='application/ld+json'> blocks via page.evaluate() + json.loads, asserted AggregateRating (@type/ratingValue/reviewCount/bestRating/worstRating), Review array shape (author.Person.name, datePublished YYYY-MM-DD regex, reviewBody non-empty, reviewRating.Rating.ratingValue/bestRating/worstRating), privacy name pattern (First L.), zero-review omission, see-all link href + label, per-card star strip count == rating, and schema↔visible review-body subset parity. The only weak spot was the live POST /checkout.php → 302 → order-success.php assertion: the page uses Stripe Elements (iframes) for card input, so direct form submission triggers client-side card validation and never POSTs. The TEST MODE 'Preview test order' button only opens a preview modal (it does NOT POST to /checkout.php). A future agent who wants to verify the 302 should either (a) drive the Stripe iframe with test card 4242 4242 4242 4242, exp 12/30, cvc 123, or (b) POST directly via requests with a session cookie carrying the cart, sending email/phone/first_name/last_name/address/city/state=CA/zip/country=US/payment_method=card form fields.
rca of the issue: No bug — feature works as specified.
